finding: Dotfiles commit-and-push is triggered manually across at least 6 sessions. The exact same prompt appears verbatim in multiple sessions: 'Check /home/jonathan/repos/dotfiles for any uncommitted changes. If there are any, run: cd /home/jonathan/repos/dotfiles && git add -A && git commit -m chore: auto-sync dotfiles && git push.' A cron agent was set up but broke due to path casing (lowercase repos vs Repos). User explicitly asked for a skill to generalize this pattern. Existing repo-autosync skill exists but is not wired up.
existing_mitigation: repo-autosync skill exists in ~/.claude/skills/ but is not connected to a working cron job. A schedule trigger was attempted but failed on path issues.

finding: At least 5 jhana sessions begin with the user asking Claude to start the emulator, test MCP, verify audio routing, or check app connectivity. start-emulator.sh exists and handles much of this, but each session still requires manual 'test the android mcp' or 'recall memory then test audio mcp' prompts. The setup has stabilized (Docker emulator, port reversal, PulseAudio sinks) but session-start verification is not automated.
existing_mitigation: start-emulator.sh script handles emulator boot, ADB connection, port reversal, and audio routing. CLAUDE.md documents the full setup. But there is no SessionStart hook or pre-flight check that auto-verifies the environment.

finding: Date night planning involves a recurring pipeline: fetch weather, check Google Calendar for existing dates, pick an idea from the ideas list, and suggest to the user. At least 5 sessions invoke this manually, each with a long pasted prompt including weather data. The user tried a scheduled remote agent (CCR) but Google Calendar MCP auth was problematic. A local cron + date-reminder-hook.py exists but only handles the nudge, not the full suggestion pipeline.
existing_mitigation: date-reminder-hook.py (UserPromptSubmit hook) nudges about date planning. dates/state.json and dates/ideas.md exist. Google Calendar MCP is connected. But the full pipeline (weather + calendar check + suggestion) is not automated end-to-end.

finding: Intender release cycle involves manually generating promotional images (via script), building a zip asset, reviewing store listing text (with humanizer/SEO skills), and uploading. At least 3 sessions cover this flow. The store deploy was commented out in CI, and asset generation requires manual invocation. Each release involves re-discovering how to generate promo images and build the zip.
existing_mitigation: A script for generating promotional images exists in the repo. CI pipeline exists but store deploy step is commented out. No single-command release workflow.

finding: Setting up the weekend project harness required manually copying the harness structure from jhana, including Android MCP configuration, emulator scripts, and CLAUDE.md patterns. Two sessions were spent on this. As more projects adopt the harness pattern, a template or scaffold command could eliminate this repeated bootstrap work.
existing_mitigation: The jhana harness serves as a reference implementation. CLAUDE.md in jhana is comprehensive. But there is no 'create new harness project' template or skill.

finding: The user explicitly stated concern about ephemeral Mint changes being lost: 'I keep doing things to mint. How can I make sure these changes are mirrored?' One session involved a full drift analysis (gsettings dump, package list, etc.) compared against NixOS config. The user wanted findings written to proposals directory. This is a recurring need as the Mint-to-NixOS migration continues — every system tweak risks being lost without detection.
existing_mitigation: A drift analyzer prompt was manually crafted and run once. No recurring automation exists. The user considered option C (periodic drift scan) but it was not implemented.
